title: phyla strigulosa var. parvifolia
intro: Phyla strigulosa var. parvifolia, also known as Mexican tea or lippia, is a plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its small leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Phyla strigulosa var. parvifolia include relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to calm the stomach, soothe respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being.
